events.title: Paul McCartney turns 84
events.blurb: Sir Paul McCartney, born on June 18, 1942, turns 84.

angles.name: Macca's Legacy
angles.copy: What's your go-to Paul McCartney track? From The Beatles to Wings and his incredible solo career, he's given us so much.

events.title: Alison Moyet turns 65
events.blurb: English singer-songwriter Alison Moyet, known for her powerful voice and work with Yazoo, was born on June 18, 1961, and turns 65 today.

angles.name: Moyet's Voice
angles.copy: Happy 65th to the incredible Alison Moyet! That voice is simply unmistakable. What's your favourite hit from her?

events.title: Lionel Richie turns 77
events.blurb: Music icon Lionel Richie, known for his solo hits and work with The Commodores, was born on June 20, 1949, and celebrates his 77th birthday.

angles.name: Hello, Lionel!
angles.copy: Is it you we're looking for? It's Lionel Richie's 77th birthday! What's your favourite song from the legend?

events.title: Cyndi Lauper turns 73
events.blurb: Grammy-winning singer Cyndi Lauper, famous for hits like 'Girls Just Want to Have Fun,' was born on June 22, 1953, and turns 73.

angles.name: Cyndi's Fun
angles.copy: She just wants to have fun! Happy 73rd birthday to the one and only Cyndi Lauper. Which of her anthems gets you dancing?

events.title: George Michael would have been 63
events.blurb: Remembering George Michael, who was born on June 25, 1963, and would have been 63 today.

angles.name: Remembering George
angles.copy: Thinking of the incredible George Michael, who would have been 63 today. His music lives on. What's your favourite track?

events.title: 17 years since Michael Jackson's passing
events.blurb: June 25, 2026, marks 17 years since the passing of the 'King of Pop,' Michael Jackson, who died on June 25, 2009, at the age of 50.

angles.name: King of Pop Tribute
angles.copy: 17 years ago today, we lost the King of Pop, Michael Jackson. His impact on music and culture is still felt worldwide. Which of his songs is timeless for you?

events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Ringo Starr, drummer for The Beatles, born on July 7, 1940, celebrates his 86th birthday.

angles.name: Peace & Love for Ringo
angles.copy: It's Ringo Starr's 86th birthday! Send him some peace and love. What's your favourite Ringo moment or Beatles track featuring him?

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist and astrophysicist, Sir Brian May, born on July 19, 1947, turns 79 today.

angles.name: Rock God's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 79th birthday to the iconic Brian May! What's your favourite Queen guitar solo or song he penned?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The Rolling Stones' frontman, Mick Jagger, born on July 26, 1943, celebrates his 83rd birthday.

angles.name: Jagger's Moves
angles.copy: Still got the moves! Happy 83rd birthday to the legendary Mick Jagger. What's your ultimate Rolling Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched 45 years ago
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its very first video.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched and changed music forever. The first video? 'Video Killed The Radio Star' by The Buggles. Poetic, isn't it?

events.title: Oasis' 'Definitely Maybe' released 32 years ago
events.blurb: Oasis released their iconic debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which became a UK number one.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Anniversary
angles.copy: It's 32 years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe'! An absolute game-changer. What's your favourite track from the album?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began 57 years ago
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal counterculture event, took place from August 15-18, 1969, on a dairy farm in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace, Love & Music
angles.copy: 57 years ago, Woodstock began: three days of peace, love, and music that defined a generation. If you could have been there, who would you have seen?

events.title: 49 years since Elvis Presley's passing
events.blurb: August 16, 2026, marks 49 years since the death of the 'King of Rock and Roll,' Elvis Presley, who passed away on August 16, 1977.

angles.name: Long Live The King
angles.copy: Remembering the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, 49 years on. His music still moves generations. What's your favourite Elvis track?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, lead singer of The Pretenders, born on September 7, 1951, celebrates her 75th birthday.

angles.name: Pretenders Icon
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the legendary Chrissie Hynde! Her voice and attitude are unmatched. What's your top Pretenders song?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Former Oasis frontman Liam Gallagher, born on September 21, 1972, celebrates his 54th birthday.

angles.name: Our Kid's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! What's your favourite track from the Oasis legend or his solo work?

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' UK single released 51 years ago
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic single 'Imagine' was released in the UK on October 24, 1975, following its US release in 1971.

angles.name: Imagine 51st
angles.copy: 51 years ago this month, John Lennon's timeless anthem 'Imagine' was released in the UK. A song that still resonates globally. What does it mean to you?

events.title: First iPod launched 25 years ago
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first-generation iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising portable music with '1,000 songs in your pocket.'

angles.name: The Original iPod
angles.copy: Remember the first iPod? 25 years ago this month, Apple put '1,000 songs in your pocket' and changed how we listen to music forever. Did you have one?
